finding: Browser-visible audio routing is a separate evidence layer from AudioContext running state, render timeline progression, physical acoustic output, and human audibility. A non-empty sinkId that matches a fulfilled setSinkId request and an enumerated audiooutput device can attest a browser-bound non-default route. An empty sinkId identifies only the user-agent default route. A route may later become unavailable without changing sinkId, so route binding cannot prove continuing physical output.
design_implication: Capture route API support, requested and observed sink identifiers, setSinkId outcome, enumerated audiooutput membership, devicechange observation, and label-exposure state in a commit-, deployment-, and session-bound packet. Preserve unsupported APIs and default routing as explicit classifications rather than treating them as proof or failure.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

uncertainty: Browser and device support for Audio Output Devices API members varies.
Device identifiers may be permission-gated or rotated.
A fulfilled route selection does not prove acoustic energy or human perception.
falsification_routes: Make a fulfilled record pass when observed sinkId differs from requested sinkId.
Make a fulfilled non-default route pass when its sink is absent from enumerateDevices.
Make an unexposed device label pass while retained in the packet.
Make the validator claim audibility or physical speaker output from route evidence alone.
privacy_boundary: Do not retain unexposed device labels, raw permission prompts, stable hardware identity beyond the session, private chat, health data, credentials, or personal identifiers.
